Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are to create a DECLARE model for the given scenario The model is a dictionary with keys representing different declarative constraints We have two groups of constraints Group 1 existence absence exactly one init Group 2 responded existence coexistence response precedence succession altresponse altprecedence altsuccession chainresponse chainprecedence chainsuccession noncoexistence nonsuccession nonchainsuccession

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For Group 1 the value is a dictionary with activities as keys and a tuple support confidence as value For Group 2 the value is a dictionary where the key is a tuple of two activities A B and the value is a tuple support confidence

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Since the problem does not specify exact values we can assume that the constraints are strong support and confidence 1 0 for rules that are absolutely necessary in the process However note that the problem says the support 1 0 and confidence so we are to set support and confidence to 1 0 for the constraints we include

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Lets think about the process Steps in order typical 1 Idea Generation IG 2 Design Draft DD 3 Technical Feasibility Check TFC 4 Cost Evaluation CE 5 Prototype Creation PC 6 Laboratory Testing LT 7 User Testing UT 8 Approval Gate AG 9 Marketing Plan MP 10 Final Launch FL

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: However note that the process may have parallel or conditional paths We are to model declarative constraints that must hold

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Each activity must occur at least once But note In a product idea not every activity may occur However the problem says Each product idea goes through a series of steps so we assume every product idea goes through all these steps

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: But wait the existence constraint with support 1 0 means that the activity must occur at least once in every trace We want to enforce that every product idea goes through these steps Then we set existence for every activity to 1 0 1 0

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: absence means the activity must NOT occur We do not have any activity that we want to forbid So we leave it empty We will set it to an empty dictionary

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: exactly one We want each activity to occur exactly once We want each product idea to go through each step exactly once So we set exactly one for every activity to 1 0 1 0

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: init The first activity must be IG So we set init for IG to 1 0 1 0

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have to define the relationships between activities response If A occurs then B must occur at some point after A precedence If B occurs then A must have occurred before B succession Both response and precedence

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We can assume the following strict sequence for a simple product launch IG to DD to TFC to CE to PC to LT to UT to AG to MP to FL

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: However note that after DD we have two parallel activities TFC and CE They can happen in any order or even in parallel But in a trace they might both occur and they both must occur before PC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So we can define DD must be followed by both TFC and CE response from DD to TFC and from DD to CE TFC and CE must both precede PC precedence for PC from TFC and from CE

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Also note that after PC we have LT and UT which might be in sequence Lets assume they are sequential LT to UT Then after UT we have AG then MP then FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then we can define response IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC PC CE PC PC LT LT UT UT AG AG MP MP FL

Name: Deductive Reasoning - NE
Text: precedence DD IG TFC DD CE DD PC TFC PC CE LT PC UT LT AG UT MP AG FL MP

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will define succession IG DD DD TFC DD CE TFC PC CE PC PC LT LT UT UT AG AG MP MP FL

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that TFC and CE can occur in any order So we cannot enforce that TFC must occur before CE or vice versa Therefore we do not want to enforce succession between TFC and CE

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We might also want to define chainresponse chainprecedence chainsuccession But chainresponse from A to B means that B must occur immediately after A We do not want to enforce that because we have parallel activities TFC and CE that might not be immediate We will avoid chain constraints for now

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also we have noncoexistence nonsuccession nonchainsuccession We do not want to enforce any of these because we do not have any forbidden patterns

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We are going to define Group 1 existence for all activities absence empty exactly one for all activities init IG Group 2 response with the listed pairs precedence with the listed pairs succession with the listed pairs We leave the other constraints as empty dictionaries

Name: Validation - PE
Text: However note that the problem says the dictionary must contain the keys So we must have all the keys
